@@ -118,7 +118,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-1xsah","title":"Add gt role command group with env-based role detection","description":"# gt role Command Group

Make role detection less fragile by using env var injection instead of cwd detection.

## Env Vars
- `GT_ROLE` - authoritative role name (mayor, witness, refinery, polecat, crew)
- `GT_ROLE_HOME` - canonical home directory for the role

## Detection Precedence
1. `$GT_ROLE` env var (authoritative)
2. Fall back to cwd-based detection (legacy/manual)
3. Warn if they disagree

## Subcommands
- `gt role` / `gt role show` - show current role
- `gt role home [ROLE]` - show home dir for role
- `gt role detect` - force cwd-based detection (debugging)
- `gt role list` - list all known roles
- `gt role env` - print export statements

## Integration Points
- gt prime - show role, warn on mismatch
- gt mol status - use gt role instead of own detection
- gt handoff - preserve GT_ROLE, reset to home
- All spawners - inject GT_ROLE and GT_ROLE_HOME

## Background
Mayor wandered to refinery dir, handoff preserved it, role detection broke.
Env var injection makes role stable across cd operations.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:50:53.378051-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.539527-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}
 {"id":"gt-1xsbn","title":"Digest: mol-deacon-patrol","description":"Patrol cycle 4: routine, healthy","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T13:22:42.747113-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:26:01.942322-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:26:01.942322-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-1y0e","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: Investigate beads-sync divergence (221 files)","description":"## Context

gt doctor now detects orphaned code on beads-sync branch. Running it shows:

  beads-sync-orphans: 221 file(s) on beads-sync not in main

This is in the gastown repo (checking from crew/max).

## Background

- Earlier today we recovered orphaned mail migration code lost in merge 96c773f
- Recently simplified architecture: all workers use mayor/rig/.beads (redirects)
- This simplification may have caused beads-sync branch to diverge unexpectedly

## Investigation Steps

1. From crew/max run: git diff main..beads-sync --stat
2. Check if beads-sync should even exist anymore (was for multi-clone sync)
3. If obsolete with redirect architecture, consider deleting it
4. If it has legitimate changes, cherry-pick or merge to main

## Key Question

With all workers using mayor/rig/.beads via redirects, is beads-sync still needed?

## Commands

git log main..beads-sync --oneline | head -20
git diff main..beads-sync -- *.go | head -100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:38:49.242099-08:00"}

All edge cases in molecular algebra can write warnings to this channel.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:26.354472-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:26.354472-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-1z4m","title":"gt swarm is implemented but undocumented","description":"internal/swarm/ package is fully implemented with:
- gt swarm create
- gt swarm status
- gt swarm list
- gt swarm land
- gt swarm cancel
- gt swarm start

But no documentation exists for it:
- Not in README
- Not in architecture.md (uses different terminology)
- Not in any role prompts

Either:
1. Document gt swarm as the canonical mechanism
2. Or deprecate it in favor of gt sling

Related to gt-6n13 (competing molecule mechanisms)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:51:23.768049-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T12:51:23.768049-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-1z4m","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:52:07.817907-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-1z4m","depends_on_id":"gt-e0qj2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-26T23:21:29.370789-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"gt-2rfvq","title":"list: static mailing list lookup","description":"Static mailing list support via config file.

## Deliverables

1. Load lists from ~/gt/config/lists.json
2. Parse list:name syntax in router
3. Fan-out at send time (same as @group)
4. Error handling for unknown list names

## Example
```bash
gt mail send list:oncall -s "Alert" -m "System down"
# Expands to: mayor/, gastown/witness, beads/witness
# Creates 3 message copies
```

## Dependencies
- Config directory (gt-i6jvc)

## Acceptance
- list:oncall resolves to configured members
- Each member gets own message copy
- Unknown list returns clear error","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:05.784822-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:05.784822-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-2rfvq","depends_on_id":"gt-i6jvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:53:05.44199-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-2sw","title":"Plugin surface for daemon lifecycle hooks","description":"Allow rigs to customize daemon behavior via hooks/plugins.

## Hook Points

- on_heartbeat: Called during daemon heartbeat cycle
- on_worker_idle: Called when worker goes idle
- on_worker_stuck: Called when stuck detection triggers
- on_lifecycle_request: Called before processing lifecycle

## Configuration

```
<rig>/config/daemon-hooks.json
{
  "on_heartbeat": "./hooks/check-ci-status.sh",
  "on_worker_idle": "./hooks/maybe-assign-work.sh",
  "idle_threshold": "5m",
  "custom_signals": [
    {"name": "ci_status", "command": "./hooks/get-ci.sh"}
  ]
}
```

## Signal Contribution

Hooks can return signals that feed into decision engine:
- SKIP_POKE: Don't poke this agent
- FORCE_POKE: Override backoff, poke now
- CUSTOM_DATA: Extra context for logging

## Use Cases

- Check CI status before interrupting test runs
- Auto-assign work when worker becomes idle
- Custom stuck detection for specific workflows","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:34.702624-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:34.702624-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-2sw","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:47.035848-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gaxo","title":"ZFC Cleanup: Move Go heuristics to Deacon molecule","description":"Remove Go code that makes workflow decisions. All health checking, staleness 
detection, nudging, and escalation belongs in the Deacon molecule where Claude 
executes it once per minute.

## The Problem

Go code currently implements polling-based health inference:
- Daemon watches heartbeats with hardcoded thresholds
- Decides if agents are "stuck" based on timestamps  
- Sends nudges, triggers restarts
- Parses mail subjects with regex to extract intent

This is a ZFC violation. Go should be message transport, not decision-maker.

## The Fix

Move all health/oversight logic to Deacon patrol molecule:
- Deacon runs once per minute
- Claude reads molecule steps, executes them
- Claude decides if agents are stuck (by checking mail, activity, etc.)
- Claude sends nudges/escalations via mail
- Go just routes messages

## Scope

1. daemon/daemon.go - heartbeat staleness logic
2. daemon/backoff.go - exponential backoff decisions
3. daemon/lifecycle.go - regex parsing of lifecycle intent
4. keepalive/keepalive.go - staleness thresholds
5. deacon/heartbeat.go - heartbeat age classification
6. doctor/stale_check.go - staleness detection
7. polecat/manager.go - state derivation from issues

## Success Criteria

- Go code has no hardcoded time.Duration for health decisions
- No regex parsing of mail subjects for intent
- No switch statements deciding agent state
- Deacon molecule has all oversight logic
- Go daemon is just a message router","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:58:18.684884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.754824-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}

 {"id":"gt-tmm","title":"Polecat beads not synced with rig beads","description":"When gt spawn assigns an issue to a polecat, the polecat cannot find the issue because:

1. Rig beads at /gt/gastown/.beads contains gt-th7
2. Polecat beads at /gt/gastown/polecats/dementus/.beads does NOT have gt-th7
3. bd sync in polecat dir pulls from beads-sync branch which doesn't have the new issue
4. Rig bd sync uses 'main' branch, causing a branch mismatch

The polecat gets a work assignment for an issue it literally cannot see.

## Root Cause

- Rig beads and polecat beads are separate git-tracked copies
- No mechanism to propagate newly created issues from rig to polecats before assignment
- Sync branch configuration mismatch between rig and polecats

## Fix Options

1. gt spawn should sync rig beads before assigning work
2. gt spawn should sync polecat beads after assignment
3. Use shared beads (symlink or same DB) instead of copies
4. Push new issues immediately on create","notes":"Note: This sync mismatch is resolved by gt-9nf (fresh polecats). Rather than fixing sync between stale clones, we'll always create fresh worktrees. This issue documents the root cause for posterity.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:17:10.158624-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.744537-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.744537-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

-{"id":"gt-tnap3","title":"queue: shared storage work queues","description":"## queue: Shared Storage Work Queues

Work queue with shared storage (single copy, first-to-claim).

## Pattern Clarification

**Queue is a MAILBOX pattern** (pull-based):
- Workers poll shared queue
- First to claim gets the work
- Worker decides when to check

**This is DIFFERENT from Dog dispatch** (push-based):
- `gt sling <work> deacon/dogs` pushes to hook
- GUPP fires immediately
- Deacon decides when to assign

| Pattern | Mechanism | Initiative |
|---------|-----------|------------|
| `queue:` | Poll mailbox, claim | Worker pulls |
| Dog sling | Hook assignment | Deacon pushes |

## Use Cases for queue:

1. **Batch cleanup tasks** - Multiple agents can help, order does not matter
2. **Overflow work** - When primary handler is busy
3. **Opportunistic tasks** - Agents check between other work

## Deliverables

1. Parse queue:name syntax in router
2. Shared storage model:
   - Single message copy (NOT fan-out)
   - Stored in queue-specific location
   - Multiple agents can see it
3. Queue configuration in ~/gt/config/queues.json
4. Queue inbox command: gt mail queue <name>
5. Claim semantics: `gt mail claim <msg-id>` marks as claimed

## Key Difference from list:
- list: creates N copies (N obligations)
- queue: creates 1 copy (1 obligation, first-to-claim)

## Dependencies
- Config directory (gt-i6jvc) ✅ closed

## Acceptance
- queue:cleanup creates single message
- Multiple agents can view queue
- Claim marks message as taken
- Message invisible to others after claim","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:07.091239-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:57:16.22733-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tnap3","depends_on_id":"gt-i6jvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:53:06.932146-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-tnca","title":"Design mol-ready-work patrol for crew workers","description":"## Summary

Design and implement a protomolecule (mol-ready-work) that enables crew workers to autonomously work through backlogs when the overseer is away. This replaces the normal "await instructions" patrol step with productive backlog processing.

## Context

Crew workers like dave/joe are persistent, user-managed agents. Currently their patrol loop has an "await instructions" step that idles. We want an alternative: sling mol-ready-work at them and they crank through backlogs until:
- The overseer interrupts with new instructions
- Context fills up (request handoff)
- Backlogs are empty

## Backlogs (Priority Order)

1. **Open PRs** - Review/merge pending pull requests
2. **Untriaged GH issues** - New issues needing triage
3. **Open beads work** - bd ready items (unblocked issues)
4. **Triaged GH issues** - Bugs/features to implement

## Key Features

### ROI-Based Selection
Instead of strict priority, agent applies ROI heuristic:
- Size estimate (fits in remaining context?)
- Achievability (has all needed info?)
- Impact (priority + type weight)
- Pick highest-value achievable item

### Context Management
- Check token usage after each work item
- Request handoff before running out
- Leave clear handoff notes (mail to self)
- Next session picks up the patrol

### Patrol Loop Structure
```
orient → scan-backlogs → select-work → execute-work → check-context → (loop or handoff)
```

## Design Questions

1. How does gt sling work? Does it exist?
2. How does this interact with the normal crew patrol?
3. Should the molecule go in Gas Town catalog or per-rig?
4. How does bonding work for discovered work during execution?
5. Should this be a wisp (ephemeral) or mol (persistent)?

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] mol-ready-work protomolecule defined in catalog
- [ ] gt sling command implemented (or existing mechanism documented)
- [ ] Context-checking step works with agent token awareness
- [ ] Handoff mechanism integrates with gt mail
- [ ] Documentation for crew workers on using the patrol","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:45:40.63509-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.309081-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.309081-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}
 {"id":"gt-tnca.1","title":"Draft mol-ready-work protomolecule definition","description":"Draft the actual protomolecule definition in markdown format.

## Molecule Structure

```markdown
## Molecule: ready-work
Autonomous backlog processing patrol for crew workers.

Phase: vapor (wisp) - ephemeral patrol cycles
Squash: after each work item or context threshold

## Step: orient
Load context and check for interrupts:
- Read mail for overseer instructions
- Check for predecessor handoff
- Load current context state

## Step: scan-backlogs
Survey all backlogs in priority order:
1. gh pr list --state open
2. gh issue list --state open --label untriaged (or no label)
3. bd ready
4. gh issue list --state open --label triaged

Capture counts and candidates.

Needs: orient

## Step: select-work
Apply ROI heuristic to select best work item:
- Estimate size (tokens needed)
- Check remaining context capacity
- Weight by impact (priority, type)
- Select highest ROI achievable item
- If empty: exit patrol

Needs: scan-backlogs

## Step: execute-work
Work the selected item:
- For PRs: review, request changes, or merge
- For untriaged: triage and label
- For beads: implement and close
- For triaged GH: implement fix

Commit, push, close/update as appropriate.

Needs: select-work

## Step: check-context
Assess context state:
- Estimate remaining capacity
- If < 20%: goto handoff
- If ok: loop to scan-backlogs

Needs: execute-work

## Step: handoff
Prepare for session transition:
- Summarize work completed this cycle
- Note any in-progress items
- Send handoff mail to self
- Squash wisp to digest
- Exit for fresh session

Needs: check-context
```

## Variables

- `backlog_priority`: Override backlog scan order
- `context_threshold`: Percentage at which to handoff (default: 20)
- `max_items`: Maximum items to process per session

## Notes

- This is a vapor-phase molecule (wisp)
- Each work item should squash to a digest
- The patrol itself squashes at handoff","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:00.012418-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.300762-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tnca.1","depends_on_id":"gt-tnca","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:00.012887-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.300762-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-tnca.2","title":"Implement context-checking for agent sessions","description":"Implement the context-checking step that allows agents to assess their remaining context capacity.

## Challenge

Agents don't have direct access to token counts. Need heuristics:

### Approach 1: Message count heuristic
- Count messages in conversation
- Estimate tokens per message
- Compare to model's context window

### Approach 2: External tool
- Claude Code could expose a /context command
- Returns estimated usage percentage
- Agent queries before each work item

### Approach 3: Conservative fixed limits
- After N work items, always handoff
- Simple but may waste context or handoff too early

### Approach 4: Hook-based injection
- SessionStart hook injects context estimate
- Updated periodically via tool call

## Recommendation

Start with Approach 3 (fixed limits) as MVP:
- Default: 3-5 work items per session
- Agent can override via variable
- Upgrade to smarter heuristics later

## Integration

- Add check-context step to mol-ready-work
- Implement handoff trigger logic
- Test with various context states","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:28.414584-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.292547-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tnca.2","depends_on_id":"gt-tnca","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:28.414964-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:56.292547-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
